Zegona Communications plc
Notification of transactions by Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibility ("PDMRs")
The Company has been informed that Suzi Williams purchased 2500 ordinary shares of £0.01 pence each in the Company ("
Ordinary Shares
") at a price of £16.8334 per share on 4 March 2026
.
Following this transaction, Suzi Williams has a total beneficial interest in the Company of 10,358 Ordinary Shares, representing approximately 0.0045 per cent. of the Company's issued share capital.

About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns. Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.

latest.title: FiberPass Transaction Completes
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 6651V
Zegona Communications PLC
06 March 2026
6 March 2026
LEI: 213800ASI1VZL2ED4S65
Zegona Communications plc
FiberPass Transaction Completes
Zegona is pleased to announce
that on 5 March 2026, AXA's1 acquisition of a 40% stake in FiberPass2 completed
.
Vodafone Spain has
generated up front proceeds of ?0.4bn from this transaction. The
ownership of FiberPass is now 55% Telef
ó
nica, 40% AXA and 5% Vodafone Spain.
?0.2
bn of the proceeds will be used to fund the share buyback programme announced on 27 November 2025.
The remaining
?0.2
bn will be used for debt reduction and puts the business in a good position to deliver net debt of only
?3.2bn by the end of our financial year, March 2026.
This reinforces Zegona's commitment to its leverage target of 1.5X - 2X and further accelerates progress in reducing total annual interest costs3.
Eamonn O'Hare, Chairman and CEO of Zegona, commented, "The AXA investment completes our transformation of Vodafone Spain's fixed network strategy. This transaction delivers significant proceeds which help drive returns to Zegona shareholders through our share buyback programme, a step change in net debt reduction and an acceleration in reducing total annual interest costs."
For further information, please contact:
Investor enquiries:
Media enquiries:
Alfonso Enríquez
Jaime De Andres
Tilly Abraham (Sodali & Co)
info
@zegona.com
jaime.andres@vodafone.com
zegona@info.sodali.com
About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns.
?
Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.
?
About Vodafone Spain
Vodafone Spain is a national provider of fixed, mobile and TV services in Spain, serving consumer, business and public administration customers. It was acquired by Zegona in 2024.
About Telef
ó
nica
Telefónica is one of the world's leading telecommunications service providers. The company offers fixed and mobile connectivity as well as a wide range of digital services for residential and business customers. With over 350 million customers, Telefónica operates in Europe and Latin America. Telefónica is listed on the Spanish stock market, New York and Lima.
Notes
1. AXA means BNPP AM Alts,
part of the BNP Paribas Group since 1st July 2025
2. FiberPass is
Compañía Mayorista de Fibra, S.L.,
the fibre joint venture between Vodafone Spain, Telefonica and BNPP AM Alts
3. Applying current market debt yields to the reduced net debt balance demonstrates the potential to drive annual interest costs well below
?200m
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: Zegona supplies fiber‑optic infrastructure and related services, mainly to telecom operators and large enterprises. Their core offering is a managed network platform that delivers high‑speed connectivity and edge computing capabilities.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The UK and European telecom markets are moving toward higher bandwidth and low‑latency services. Zegona’s platform sits at the intersection of fiber rollout and edge computing, giving it a niche in a sector that is expanding rapidly as 5G and cloud services grow.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a rebuilding phase. After a recent share cancellation and a series of governance changes, it has started a small dividend to signal confidence. Its valuation is heavily discounted compared to its peers, reflecting a long‑term recovery path.

rns_narrative: Zegona has been tightening its board and ownership structure, selling off a large block of shares and completing a FiberPass transaction that should improve cash flow.
The company’s inclusion in the MSCI UK Index is a sign of growing institutional interest, even as it pushes a modest dividend to attract new investors.
The most recent filings show a small transaction in own shares and a change in voting rights, suggesting management is consolidating control while keeping the firm on a stable footing.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism after dividend chatter – investors are waiting for a clear rebound.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls see the dividend as a catalyst and the MSCI promotion as validation of a long‑term turnaround.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the share cancellation and heavy governance changes could lead to a fragile ownership structure and that the dividend might not be sustainable.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has moved from indifference to cautious optimism; the community is still silent on a concrete turnaround but is primed for a surprise move.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no activity – the stock is invisible to the crowd, which means any news or earnings will hit like a hammer.

near_term_catalysts: The ex‑dividend date on 9 January is a natural buying window; if the price stays above support, the dividend could spark a rally.
Completion of the FiberPass transaction is expected to reduce debt and improve margins – a fundamental boost.
Inclusion in the MSCI index will trigger passive inflows; a modest price bump could trigger momentum.
Any announcement of new fibre‑optic contracts or a partnership with a major telecom operator would be a clear upside trigger.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 3‑day spike in volume that exceeds 1.5× the 20‑day average and a bounce above the 200‑day moving average. A clear break of the 30‑day low will confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is likely in the early stages of a long‑term recovery. The APEX score indicates a tight compression, suggesting that a small catalyst could unlock a large move. Confirmation will come when the price moves out of the deep‑crash zone and starts trading above short‑term support.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company is trading after a 70‑plus percent drop from its peak. If the price rebounds to the halfway point of that decline, the upside is roughly 35‑45% relative to the current level – a classic asymmetric payoff. The dividend, MSCI inclusion, and a completed fibre transaction all add layers of confidence that the fundamentals are stabilising.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the dividend is a one‑off and that the company’s cash burn could resume if the fibre pipeline stalls. A further 10‑20% drop would wipe out the upside and leave a small margin for error.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 30% if the price falls another 30% from today. Potential gain: 40% if the price recovers to the halfway mark. The upside is more than double the downside, giving a 1:2 risk‑reward ratio that favours a contrarian long.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a clear break above the 200‑day moving average and a volume spike of at least 1.5× the 20‑day average. Size the position so that a 30% move against you would trigger a stop at the 30‑day low. Use a trailing stop to lock in gains once the price moves beyond the halfway rebound.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the ex‑dividend date and the next earnings report. Watch for a 3‑day volume surge and a price bounce above the 200‑day average. Only then consider a small position to test the mean‑reversion.
playbook.if_passing: None identified. The company shows no signs of delisting, bankruptcy, or fraud. The setup remains bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Zegona is a deep‑crashed play with a clear dividend and index promotion as catalysts – a 40% upside to the halfway point, with a 30% downside risk, making it a classic contrarian long.
